title: carex alata var. ferruginea
intro: Carex alata var. ferruginea, also known as the golden sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family. It is native to Europe and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The golden sedge is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, thanks to its expectorant properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular choice for natural remedies.
